3	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 9	Subject Line Matrix
Subject Line
MATRIX
Overview
(00:00)
All right, so we’re talking about how to write subject lines and ad creatives.
This is something that can frankly be used for headlines as well actually so it
relates to subject lines and headlines and what we’re going to be going over
is what I call my subject line matrix but again, this could just as easily be
headline matrix as well. Basically, what we’re doing is we have this grid here
and we’re going to go through and we’re going to pull out stuff from different
what are the sales copies that are going to write headlines for or that we’re
going to write subject lines for and pull different elements out.
(00:47)
So you can see what are the promises, what are the problems I have to
dimensionalize? Remember, dimensionalize means it’s not just don’t have
money, but it’s always feeling panicked at a restaurant when the bill comes
because you’re really hoping that your credit card won’t get declined. So it’s
making it more dimensionalized, making it deeper. If there was a new cause
discovered of the problem. If there’s a new mechanism, remember so it turns
out that the secret to losing weight is not this, but it’s this or the secret to
getting ahead of your retirement investing isn’t to do this, but it’s to do this.
(01:28)
If the well-known remedy or solution is wrong or bad, we’re going to note that
from the copy. The well-known problem or taboo is really a solution, so if the
copy that you’re pulling out subject lines and headlines for is saying about
how eating bacon helps to reduce blood pressure that would go there. Or why
putting your money in an RRA is retirement suicide; if that was something that
was in some copy of your writing, you put it there.
(01:58)
Dominant emotion, so what are the emotions that are going to be in here like
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 8 --

4	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 9	Subject Line Matrix
sadness, happiness, joy, whatever the feelings are putting them there. The
story, so whatever the story is from the sales letter or the sales copy or any
other story, you pull it and put it here.
(02:15)
Conflict, controversy, conspiracy, challenge, shock, anything like Big Pharma
doesn’t want you to know this or controversial method for investing or CNN
called him the most hated man on Wall Street, things like that would be
interesting there.
(02:34)
You have this problem of the normal solution I guess, this goes with the well-
known remedies, wrong or bad, to be honest with you. In fact, I feel like you
know what, I’m going to do in real time, I’m going to just remove this because
really, that’s like the same thing, which is actually good because then it gives
us a little more space to work with which is actually going to be nice.
(02:57)
People, so people are mentioned in the sales copy like a doctor, lawyer, the
cousin, the name of the avatar, spokesperson, whatever it is. Topical like
right now, yeah, there’s topical stuff in the sales letter, but also there’s topical
stuff going on that you could build around. For example, COVID-19 right now
is topical, debt stimulus, it’s topical, after the Super Bowl 2020, people are
talking about Shakira and J. Lo showing too much skin or whatever and this
whole debate, that would be topical so whatever it is.
(03:36)
Believability, it really could be credibility, same thing. Credibility factors that
are in the sales copy. Urgency or scarcity, is there any limited time only, 15
copies only, 100 copies only, limited supplies and then targeted would be is
the copy targeted to a specific demographic like 30 to 45 year old women or
25 to 29 year old men or whatever it is targeted.
(04:06)
So again, we’re just basically going to pull stuff from the sales letter and that’s
what we’re going to do there. I’ll stop it here, this one just being the overview,
and we’ll do some examples.
